info.title: eBPFsentinel Agent API
info.description:
REST API for the eBPFsentinel network security agent.

Provides endpoints for managing firewall rules, IPS/IDS policies, rate limiting, L7 filtering, threat intelligence, alerts, and audit logs.

Most endpoints require authentication via JWT Bearer token or API key.

get.summary:
Returns the agent's identity metadata, including the operator-managed
flag and the optional operator UI deep-link URL.
get.description:
Hot-reloadable: a config reload that toggles `management.operator_managed`
or updates `management.operator_endpoint` is reflected on the next
request without restart.

get.summary: Returns the current agent status including version, uptime, and eBPF state.
`GET /api/v1/alerts` — query stored alerts with optional filters.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of time range (nanoseconds since epoch, inclusive).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of time range (nanoseconds since epoch, inclusive).

parameters.name: technique
parameters.description: Filter by MITRE ATT&CK technique ID (e.g. "T1041").

get.description:
Server-side filtering happens on every alert before it is forwarded.
Reconnects can pass `Last-Event-ID` to backfill missed alerts from
the in-memory replay buffer (≤ 5 000 events). Lagged subscribers
silently skip the gap; clients should refetch via
`GET /api/v1/alerts` to backfill in that case.

200.description: SSE stream of `event: alert` frames; `:keepalive` every 15 s

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/alerts/{id}/false-positive` — mark an alert as false positive.

put.summary: `PUT /api/v1/aliases/{id}/content` — push content for an External alias.

400.description: Invalid content or alias is not External

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of time range (nanoseconds since epoch, inclusive).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of time range (nanoseconds since epoch, inclusive).

post.summary: Trigger a configuration reload via the API.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: Reverse lookup: find domains for this IP.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/domains/blocklist` — add a domain to the runtime blocklist.

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/domains/blocklist/{domain}` — remove a domain from the runtime blocklist.

get.summary: Return the load status of each eBPF program.

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/firewall/l7-rules/:id` — delete an L7 rule by ID.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: IP address to resolve (IPv4 or IPv6).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/ips/blacklist` — manually add an IP to the IPS blacklist.
post.description:
Drives the same `add_to_blacklist` path the DNS-blocklist and reputation
auto-block features already use, so the entry is visible via `GET` and
subject to the policy's TTL cap + expiry sweeper.

400.description: Invalid IP address or blacklist full

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/ips/blacklist/{ip}` — remove an IP from the IPS blacklist.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/ips/domain-blocks` — list IPS blacklist entries originating from domain mechanisms.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/lb/vips` — apply a new VIP announce configuration.

200.description: Coverage matrix of covered ATT&CK techniques

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/ratelimit/rules` — create a new rate limit rule.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/routing/routes` — effective default route(s) from gateway selection.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/threatintel/feeds/refresh` — trigger an immediate re-fetch
of all enabled threat-intel feeds.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/threatintel/urls` — list malicious URL indicators ingested
from CTI feeds. The threat-intel engine is IP-only, so URL indicators are
surfaced from the service's retained snapshot.

`GET /api/v1/tls/status` — report the negotiated TLS key-exchange group
for the current connection.

200.description: Negotiated TLS parameters for this connection

get.summary: Liveness probe — always returns 200 if the process is running.
Serves Prometheus metrics in `OpenMetrics` text format.
Readiness probe — returns 200 when eBPF programs are loaded, 503 otherwise.

ip.description: IP address to blacklist (IPv4 or IPv6).

ttl_secs.description:
Optional TTL in seconds. Defaults to — and is capped at — the policy's
maximum blacklist duration.

AgentIdentityResponse.description:
Operator-managed metadata advertised to the dashboard, plus the
minimum identity fields needed to identify the agent uniquely.

Read-only view of the agent's `management` config block; both fields
are hot-reloadable via the existing config reload mechanism.

hostname.description: `gethostname()` at startup; surfaces the host running the agent.

operator_endpoint.description:
Optional URL the operator exposes (typically a Kubernetes-native
UI). The dashboard deep-links to this URL from the
"operator-managed" badge.

operator_managed.description:
`true` when the agent's configuration is owned by the Kubernetes
operator (CRD-driven). The dashboard locks its config-edit UI on
agents reporting `true` to prevent two-way drift.

version.description: Build version of the running agent (`CARGO_PKG_VERSION`).

status.description:
Coarse lifecycle state: `running` once eBPF programs are attached,
`degraded` while operating without them (userspace-only fallback).

oneOf.description: Container identity resolved from the event's `cgroup_id` (if any).

data_type.description: DLP: data category (pci, pii, credentials, custom).

src_addr.description:
Source address as four big-endian u32 words.
IPv4: `[v4, 0, 0, 0]`. IPv6: full 128-bit address.

max_connections.description:
Conntrack table capacity — the maximum number of flows the
agent tracks before eviction (the BPF connection-table size).

ContainerIdentity.description:
Container provenance surfaced on an alert. `kind` is `container` for a
resolved container and `host` for a host-namespace process; the Docker /
Kubernetes fields are populated only when an enricher attached metadata.

container_name.description: Kubernetes container name (only when a k8s enricher attached metadata).

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace (only when a k8s enricher attached metadata).

pod.description: Kubernetes pod name (only when a k8s enricher attached metadata).

runtime.description: Detected runtime (docker, containerd, crio, podman, unknown).

health_check_interval_secs.description: Health-check probe interval in seconds. When set, an ICMP probe is configured.

interface.description: Egress interface; defaults to empty (inherits the agent's primary).

weight.description: Routing weight; maps to failover priority (lower = preferred).

CreateNptV6RuleRequest.description: Request DTO for creating an `NPTv6` prefix translation rule.

rate_pps.description: Rate limit in packets per second (required for `throttle_ip`).

action.description: `allow` permits traffic; any other verb denies.

interfaces.description: Network interfaces grouped into this zone.

name.description: Zone name; used as the zone identifier.

subnets.description: Subnets are accepted for forward-compatibility but not yet enforced.

enabled.description: Whether DNS intelligence (cache + blocklist) is enabled.

ips.description: IP addresses/CIDRs to load into the alias.

last_fetched.description:
Unix-epoch milliseconds of the last completed feed fetch, or `null`
if no fetch has run yet. Shared across feeds (one fetch cycle).

persistent.description: Whether the cache is backed by a persistent store that survives restarts.

health_status.description: Alias of `status` for clients expecting a `health_status` field.

id.description: Gateway identifier, rendered as a string for stable API addressing.

weight.description: Alias of `priority`, exposed as a routing weight.

enabled.description: Whether `GeoIP` enrichment is enabled in the agent configuration.

ready.description: Whether an mmdb-backed lookup database is loaded and ready.

cached_count.description: Number of cached JA4S server fingerprints.

persistent.description: Whether the cache is backed by a persistent store that survives restarts.

interface.description: L2 interface VIPs egress on. Required when role is primary/standby.

vips.description:
VIPs this node owns. Must be non-empty when role is
primary/standby; ignored when role is disabled.

arp_replies.description:
Forged ARP replies emitted for this VIP since program load
(per-CPU summed kernel counter).

self_announced.description:
Whether this VIP is currently in the self-whitelist (true when
the node is speaker and the binding has been written).

bindings_count.description:
Number of self-owned bindings registered in the kernel
`SELF_OWNED_BINDINGS` map.

interface.description: L2 interface VIPs egress on (empty when role is disabled).

is_speaker.description: Whether this node currently answers ARP / sends gratuitous ARP.

total_techniques.description: Total number of covered techniques (for active components).

NptV6RuleResponse.description: Response DTO for an `NPTv6` prefix translation rule.

country_codes.description:
ISO-3166 country codes this rule is scoped to (userspace annotation;
enforced by the GeoIP-aware ratelimit pipeline). Absent when unscoped.

ebpf_loaded.description: Whether eBPF programs are successfully loaded.

RefreshFeedRequest.description: Optional body for a feed refresh request.

feed_id.description:
Feed to refresh. Accepted for forward compatibility; the fetcher
currently refreshes every enabled feed in a single cycle.

RefreshResponse.description: Response to a feed refresh request.

RouteResponse.description: A resolved default route derived from the active gateway selection.

destination.description: Destination CIDR served by this route.

gateway_id.description: Identifier of the gateway this route egresses through.

country_codes.description:
ISO-3166 country codes this rule matches, resolved from a `GeoIP`
alias referenced by `src_alias`/`dst_alias`. Set only when the
referenced alias is a `GeoIP` kind.

snap_length.description: Snap length: max bytes per packet (default 1500).

TechniqueEntry.description: A single technique in the coverage matrix.

ThresholdResponse.description: Per-rule threshold/rate detection, surfaced for rate-based IDS rules.

TlsStatusResponse.description:
Per-connection TLS status, surfaced so operators can confirm the
negotiated key-exchange group (e.g. verify a post-quantum hybrid
handshake) on a live connection.

negotiated_group.description:
Negotiated TLS 1.3 key-exchange named group for this connection
(e.g. `X25519MLKEM768`, `X25519`, `secp256r1`), or `null` when the
request did not arrive over TLS.

post_quantum.description:
`true` when the negotiated group is the post-quantum hybrid
(`X25519MLKEM768`).

tls.description: Whether this request arrived over TLS.

action.description: Alias of `policy`, exposed under the `action` field name.

api_key.name: X-API-Key
api_key.description: Static API key configured in agent

bearer_auth.description: JWT token obtained via OIDC or static configuration

tags.name: Audit
tags.description: Audit log queries and rule history

tags.name: Threat Intelligence
tags.description: Threat intel feeds and IOC status

tags.name: Ops
tags.description: Operational endpoints: config reload, eBPF status

tags.name: DNS
tags.description: DNS cache, stats, and blocklist management

tags.name: DDoS
tags.description: DDoS detection, mitigation, and policy management

tags.name: DLP
tags.description: Data loss prevention status and patterns

tags.name: QoS
tags.description: Traffic shaping: pipes, queues, and classifiers

tags.name: MITRE ATT&CK
tags.description: MITRE ATT&CK coverage and technique mapping

tags.name: Fingerprints
tags.description: JA4+ TLS fingerprint cache and analysis

tags.name: Responses
tags.description: Manual time-bounded response actions (block/throttle with TTL)
